metadata.note: Tech Mahindra focuses on practical problem-solving, moderate difficulty, with emphasis on edge cases, optimization, and clean code. No overlap with previous sets.

questions.title: Find All Duplicates in an Array (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Given an array of integers where 1 ≤ a[i] ≤ n, some appear twice, others once. Find all elements that appear twice.
options: A) Use hash set O(n) space
B) Mark visited by negating value at index nums[i]-1
C) Sort and scan O(n log n)
D) Both B and using negation in O(n) time O(1) space
questions.correct_answer: D) Both B and using negation in O(n) time O(1) space
questions.explanation: When encountering nums[i], check if nums[abs(nums[i])-1] is negative; if yes, duplicate; else negate.

questions.title: Maximum Subarray Sum (Kadane's Algorithm, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find the contiguous subarray with the largest sum.
options: B) O(n) Kadane's algorithm: maxEndingHere = max(num, maxEndingHere+num)
C) Divide and conquer O(n log n)
D) Both B and C are valid
questions.correct_answer: D) Both B and C are valid
questions.explanation: Kadane's is O(n) and most efficient; divide and conquer also works but slower.

questions.title: Container With Most Water (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find two lines that together with x-axis form a container holding most water.
options: B) Two pointers starting from ends, moving the shorter line inward
D) Both B and O(n) approach
questions.correct_answer: D) Both B and O(n) approach
questions.explanation: Two-pointer O(n) solution: area = min(height[l], height[r]) * (r-l); move pointer with smaller height.

questions.title: 3Sum (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find all triplets that sum to zero, no duplicates.
options: B) Sort, then fix one element and use two-pointer for other two
C) Use hash map to store pairs
D) Both B and C, B is O(n^2) time O(1) space
questions.correct_answer: D) Both B and C, B is O(n^2) time O(1) space
questions.explanation: Sort array, for each i, use left and right pointers to find target = -nums[i].

questions.title: Sort Colors (Dutch National Flag, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Sort array containing 0,1,2 in-place.
options: B) One-pass three pointers (low, mid, high)
C) Sorting algorithm O(n log n)
D) Both A and B, B is one-pass
questions.correct_answer: D) Both A and B, B is one-pass
questions.explanation: Three pointers: low for 0, mid for 1, high for 2; swap accordingly.

questions.title: Next Permutation (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Rearrange numbers into lexicographically next greater permutation.
options: B) Find first decreasing element from right, swap with next greater, reverse suffix
D) Both B and standard O(n) algorithm
questions.correct_answer: D) Both B and standard O(n) algorithm
questions.explanation: Step 1: find a[i] < a[i+1]; step 2: find a[j] > a[i] from right; step 3: swap; step 4: reverse suffix.

questions.title: Product of Array Except Self (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Return array where output[i] = product of all elements except nums[i], without division.
options: B) Use prefix and suffix products
C) Use division with special zero handling
D) Both B and O(n) time O(1) space
questions.correct_answer: D) Both B and O(n) time O(1) space
questions.explanation: Left pass: prefix product; right pass: multiply by suffix product.

questions.title: Find First and Last Position of Element in Sorted Array (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find starting and ending index of target in sorted array.
options: B) Two binary searches: leftmost and rightmost
C) Single binary search then expand
D) Both B and standard log n solution
questions.correct_answer: D) Both B and standard log n solution
questions.explanation: Binary search for left bound (lower bound) and right bound (upper bound -1).

questions.title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Search target in rotated sorted array (distinct values).
options: B) Find pivot then binary search
C) Modified binary search checking which half is sorted
D) Both B and C are O(log n)
questions.correct_answer: D) Both B and C are O(log n)
questions.explanation: Compare nums[mid] with nums[left] to determine which half is sorted, then search in appropriate half.

questions.title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find median of two sorted arrays in O(log(m+n)).
options: B) Binary search on smaller array partitioning
D) Both B and optimal solution
questions.correct_answer: D) Both B and optimal solution
questions.explanation: Partition smaller array such that left half elements <= right half; condition: max(left) <= min(right).

questions.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find length of longest substring without repeating characters.
options: C) Sliding window with hash map (last index)
D) Both B and C achieve O(n)
questions.correct_answer: D) Both B and C achieve O(n)
questions.explanation: Two pointers: expand right, if char seen, move left to max(left, lastSeen[char]+1).

questions.title: Longest Palindromic Substring (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find longest palindromic substring.
options: B) DP O(n^2) time and space
questions.explanation: Expand around center is simplest; Manacher's is linear but complex.

questions.title: Valid Parentheses (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Check if string of brackets is valid.
options: A) Use stack, push opening, pop on closing, check match
C) Use counter for each type
questions.explanation: Stack ensures correct nesting order.

questions.title: Generate Parentheses (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Generate all combinations of n pairs of well-formed parentheses.
options: A) Backtracking with open and close counts
C) DP using Catalan numbers then generate
D) Both A and standard backtracking
questions.correct_answer: D) Both A and standard backtracking
questions.explanation: Recursion: add '(' if open < n, add ')' if close < open.

questions.title: Merge Two Sorted Lists (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Merge two sorted linked lists into one sorted list.
options: A) Create new list by comparing heads
C) Append and sort O(n log n)
D) Both A and B are O(m+n)
questions.correct_answer: D) Both A and B are O(m+n)
questions.explanation: Two-pointer iterative or recursive both work.

questions.title: Add Two Numbers (Linked List, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Add two numbers represented as reverse-order linked lists.
options: A) Convert to int, add, convert back
B) Traverse both lists with carry propagation
D) Both B and standard solution
questions.correct_answer: D) Both B and standard solution
questions.explanation: Simultaneous traversal with carry; handle remaining digits.

questions.title: Reverse Linked List (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Reverse a singly linked list.
options: D) Both A and B are O(n)
questions.correct_answer: D) Both A and B are O(n)
questions.explanation: Iterative: prev, curr, next; recursive: reverse rest then adjust.

questions.title: Detect Cycle in Linked List (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Determine if linked list has a cycle.
options: A) Floyd's cycle detection (tortoise and hare)
B) Hash set of visited nodes
C) Mark nodes as visited by modifying
D) Both A and B are valid
questions.correct_answer: D) Both A and B are valid
questions.explanation: Floyd's O(1) space; hash set O(n) space.

questions.title: Remove Nth Node From End of List (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Remove nth node from end of linked list.
options: A) Compute length, then traverse to len-n-1
B) Two pointers with gap of n
D) Both A and B are O(n)
questions.correct_answer: D) Both A and B are O(n)
questions.explanation: One-pass with two pointers: move first n steps, then both until first reaches end.

questions.title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Return level order traversal of binary tree.
options: D) Use stack for reverse level
questions.explanation: BFS processes level by level; DFS can also store nodes by level in a map.

questions.title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find maximum depth (height) of binary tree.
questions.explanation: All approaches work; BFS levels, DFS recursion, or iterative stack.

questions.title: Validate Binary Search Tree (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Determine if binary tree is a valid BST.
options: A) In-order traversal and check if sorted
D) Check left < root < right only (fails for deep violations)
questions.explanation: In-order gives sorted order; bounds method ensures all nodes in range.

questions.title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find maximum path sum (path can start/end at any node).
options: B) DFS returning max gain from node to leaf, updating global max
C) Use Kadane's algorithm on flattened tree
D) Both B and optimal solution
questions.correct_answer: D) Both B and optimal solution
questions.explanation: Post-order: compute left and right gains, update global with left+right+node.val, return node.val + max(leftGain, rightGain, 0).

questions.title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find lowest common ancestor of two given nodes.
options: A) Find paths from root to p and q, then compare
B) Recursive: if root in {p,q} return root; left=LCA(left), right=LCA(right); if both non-null return root
questions.explanation: Recursive solution: if left and right both non-null, current is LCA.

questions.title: Number of Islands (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count number of islands in 2D grid.
options: A) DFS/BFS each '1' and mark visited
questions.explanation: DFS/BFS is standard; union-find also works.

questions.title: Clone Graph (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Deep copy of undirected graph.
options: A) BFS with queue and map
questions.explanation: Map original node to clone node; BFS/DFS to traverse and copy neighbors.

questions.title: Course Schedule (Cycle Detection, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Determine if all courses can be finished given prerequisites.
options: A) DFS with three states (unvisited, visiting, visited)
questions.explanation: Kahn's: remove nodes with indegree 0, if some remain then cycle.

questions.title: Word Search (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Check if word exists in grid (adjacent cells, no reuse).
options: A) DFS from each cell with backtracking
D) Use trie for multiple words
questions.explanation: DFS with visited set and backtracking is standard.

questions.title: Climbing Stairs (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Number of ways to climb n steps taking 1 or 2 steps.
questions.explanation: DP bottom-up O(n); matrix expo O(log n) for large n.

questions.title: Coin Change (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum coins to make amount, return -1 if impossible.
options: A) Greedy fails for some denominations
questions.explanation: Unbounded knapsack DP O(amount * coins); BFS also works.

questions.title: Longest Common Subsequence (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Length of longest common subsequence of two strings.
options: B) DP: if match, 1+dp[i-1][j-1]; else max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1])
C) Both B and O(mn) DP
questions.correct_answer: C) Both B and O(mn) DP
questions.explanation: Standard 2D DP table; space can be optimized to O(min(m,n)).

questions.title: House Robber (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max sum without robbing adjacent houses.
options: B) Two variables for previous two states
questions.explanation: DP O(n) time O(1) space using prev, prevPrev.

questions.title: Target Sum (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Number of ways to assign +/- to reach target sum.
options: A) Subset sum: sum(P) - sum(N) = target => sum(P) = (total+target)/2
B) DP with offset for negative indices
questions.explanation: Transform to count subsets with sum = (total+target)/2; DP O(n*sum).

questions.title: Permutations (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Return all permutations of distinct integers.
questions.explanation: Swap-based or used-array both generate O(n!) permutations.

questions.title: Subsets (Power Set, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Return all subsets of unique elements.
options: A) Cascading: start with [[]], for each num, extend
questions.explanation: Three classical approaches: iterative cascading, recursion, bitmask.

questions.title: Combination Sum (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find all combinations that sum to target (can reuse same number).
questions.explanation: Recursion: for each index, include number, recurse from same index (allow reuse).

questions.title: Implement Trie (Prefix Tree, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Implement insert, search, startsWith methods.
options: A) Node with children[26] and isEnd
questions.explanation: Array for fixed lowercase letters, map for sparse/unicode.

questions.title: Top K Frequent Elements (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Return k most frequent elements.
options: A) Frequency map + sort O(n log n)
B) Min-heap of size k O(n log k)
questions.explanation: Bucket sort (frequency as index) is O(n).

questions.title: Kth Largest Element in Array (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find kth largest element.
options: B) Min-heap of size k O(n log k)
questions.explanation: Quick select (Hoare's partition) has O(n) average.

questions.title: Group Anagrams (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Group strings that are anagrams.
options: A) Sort each string and use as key
B) Count frequency and use tuple as key
questions.explanation: Sorted string or character count tuple as key in hash map.

questions.title: Valid Anagram (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Check if two strings are anagrams.
questions.explanation: Frequency array is simplest O(n) time O(1) space.

questions.title: First Unique Character in String (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find first non-repeating character and return its index.
options: A) Two passes: first count, second find count=1
B) Use queue to track order
questions.explanation: Two-pass count is simple O(n); queue works for streaming.

questions.title: Majority Element (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find element appearing more than n/2 times.
questions.explanation: Boyer-Moore: candidate and count; cancels different elements.

questions.title: Missing Number (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find missing number in array 0..n.
options: B) XOR all numbers 0..n and XOR all array elements
D) Both A and B O(n) time O(1) space
questions.correct_answer: D) Both A and B O(n) time O(1) space
questions.explanation: XOR avoids overflow; sum approach may overflow for large n.

questions.title: Single Number (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find element that appears once, others twice.
options: D) Both A and B, A is O(1) space
questions.correct_answer: D) Both A and B, A is O(1) space
questions.explanation: XOR cancels duplicates: a⊕a=0.

questions.title: Reverse Bits (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Reverse bits of 32-bit unsigned integer.
options: A) Loop 32 times, extract LSB and shift into result
B) Use lookup table for byte reversal
questions.explanation: Bit iteration O(32); lookup table trades space for speed.

questions.title: Number of 1 Bits (Hamming Weight, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count number of 1 bits in unsigned integer.
options: A) Check each bit by shifting
B) n & (n-1) trick: clears lowest set bit each time
questions.explanation: n & (n-1) runs in O(number of set bits).

questions.title: Pow(x, n) (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Implement pow(x, n) (x raised to power n).
options: B) Fast exponentiation (binary exponentiation) O(log n)
questions.explanation: Fast exponentiation: if n even, x^n = (x^(n/2))^2; if odd, x * x^(n-1).

questions.title: Sqrt(x) (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Compute integer square root of x.
questions.explanation: Binary search finds largest mid where mid*mid ≤ x.

questions.title: Roman to Integer (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Convert Roman numeral to integer.
options: A) Process left to right: if current < next, subtract; else add
B) Use hash map for symbols
D) Replace subtractive combos then sum
questions.explanation: Key rule: I before V = 4, etc. So if value(current) < value(next), subtract.

questions.title: Palindrome Number (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Determine if integer is palindrome without converting to string.
options: B) Convert to string and compare
C) Both A and B, A is O(1) space
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(1) space
questions.explanation: Reverse half: revertNum = revertNum*10 + x%10; x/=10; stop when revertNum >= x.

questions.title: Fizz Buzz (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Return list of strings from 1 to n, multiples of 3 Fizz, 5 Buzz, both FizzBuzz.
questions.explanation: Standard modulo check O(n).

questions.title: Plus One (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Add one to array of digits representing integer.
options: A) Convert to int, add, convert back risks overflow
B) Process from rightmost with carry
questions.explanation: Start from end; if digit < 9, increment and return; else set to 0 and carry. If carry remains, insert 1 at front.

questions.title: Move Zeroes (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Move all zeros to end while preserving order of non-zero elements.
options: A) Create new array O(n) space
B) Two-pointer: swap non-zero with first zero position
D) Both B and in-place O(n)
questions.correct_answer: D) Both B and in-place O(n)
questions.explanation: Pointer lastNonZero = 0; iterate, if nums[i] != 0, swap with nums[lastNonZero] and increment.

questions.title: Two Sum (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Return indices of two numbers that add to target.
options: B) Use hash map for complement O(n)
questions.explanation: Hash map gives O(n) time O(n) space.

questions.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Maximum profit from one buy and one sell.
options: A) Track min price so far and max profit
questions.explanation: Single pass: minPrice = min(minPrice, price); profit = max(profit, price - minPrice).

questions.title: Contains Duplicate (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Return true if any value appears at least twice.
questions.explanation: Hash set O(n) time O(n) space; sort O(n log n) O(1) space.

questions.title: Merge Intervals (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Merge overlapping intervals.
options: A) Sort by start, then merge if current.end >= next.start
C) Both A and B, A is simpler
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is simpler
questions.explanation: Sort O(n log n), linear merge.

questions.title: Insert Interval (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Insert new interval into sorted non-overlapping intervals and merge.
options: A) Find insertion position, merge overlapping
B) Add interval to list, sort, and merge
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Single pass: add intervals ending before new.start, then merge overlapping, then add remaining.

questions.title: Non-overlapping Intervals (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum intervals to remove to make rest non-overlapping.
options: A) Sort by end, greedy keep intervals with earliest end
B) Sort by start, LIS-like DP
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.explanation: Greedy by earliest end yields max non-overlapping intervals; remove = n - kept.

questions.title: Meeting Rooms II (Tech Mahindra, concept)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum number of meeting rooms required.
options: A) Sort intervals, use min-heap for end times
B) Separate start and end arrays, sweep line
C) Both A and B O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B O(n log n)
questions.explanation: Heap tracks earliest ending meeting; sweep line uses +1 at start, -1 at end.

questions.title: Task Scheduler (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Least CPU units to finish tasks with cooldown n.
options: A) Greedy using max-heap of frequencies
C) Both A and B, B is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, B is O(n)
questions.explanation: Formula works when no idle cycle needed; simulation handles all cases.

questions.title: Gas Station (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find starting gas station to complete circuit.
options: B) Greedy: if total gas < total cost return -1; start where tank never negative
questions.explanation: If total >= total cost, solution exists; start from station where cumulative sum minimal.

questions.title: Candy (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum candies to distribute with ratings, higher rating gets more than neighbor.
options: A) Two-pass: left to right, right to left
questions.explanation: Left pass ensures increase from left; right pass ensures from right.

questions.title: Partition Labels (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Partition string into as many parts as possible so each letter appears in at most one part.
options: A) Store last occurrence of each char, expand segment end
B) Greedy: as soon as segment end reached, cut
questions.explanation: O(n) scan: track last index of each char; expand current segment until i reaches segment end; then cut.

questions.title: Subarray Sum Equals K (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count number of subarrays summing to k.
options: B) Prefix sum + hash map: count[prefixSum]++
D) Both B and O(n) approach
questions.correct_answer: D) Both B and O(n) approach
questions.explanation: Prefix sum: for each i, find prefixSum - k in map; add to count.

questions.title: Subarray Product Less Than K (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count subarrays with product < k.
options: A) Sliding window: expand right, shrink left if product >= k, add window size to count
D) Both A and sliding window O(n)
questions.correct_answer: D) Both A and sliding window O(n)
questions.explanation: For each right, find largest left where product < k; count = right-left+1.

questions.title: Maximum Product Subarray (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Maximum product subarray (handles negatives).
options: A) Kadane variant tracking max and min
D) Both A and optimal O(n)
questions.correct_answer: D) Both A and optimal O(n)
questions.explanation: At each step, max = max(nums[i], max*nums[i], min*nums[i]); min similarly.

questions.title: Longest Consecutive Sequence (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Length of longest consecutive elements sequence in O(n).
options: B) Use hash set, for each num check if num-1 exists (start of sequence)
questions.explanation: Put all in set; for each num, if num-1 not in set, count streak, update max.

questions.title: Find the Duplicate Number (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find duplicate in array of n+1 numbers (1..n) without extra space.
options: A) Floyd's cycle detection (treat array as linked list)
B) Binary search on value range O(n log n)
C) Sorting O(n log n) modifies
D) Both A and O(n) time O(1) space
questions.correct_answer: D) Both A and O(n) time O(1) space
questions.explanation: nums[i] points to index; duplicate creates cycle; Floyd's finds cycle start.

questions.title: Rotate Array (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Rotate array right by k steps in-place.
options: A) Reverse entire, reverse first k, reverse remaining
D) Both A and O(n) in-place
questions.correct_answer: D) Both A and O(n) in-place
questions.explanation: Three reverses: reverse(0,n-1), reverse(0,k-1), reverse(k,n-1).

questions.title: Spiral Matrix (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Return matrix elements in spiral order.
options: A) Four boundaries (top, bottom, left, right)
B) Simulate direction changes with visited
questions.explanation: Boundary shrinking is simpler: while top<=bottom and left<=right.

questions.title: Set Matrix Zeroes (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Set entire row and column to zero if element is 0, in-place O(1) space.
options: A) Use first row and column as markers
B) Separate row/col arrays O(m+n) space
C) Both A and B, A is O(1) space
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(1) space
questions.explanation: First pass: mark zeros in first row/col; second pass: set cells using markers; handle first row/col separately.

questions.title: Rotate Image (Matrix, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Rotate n x n matrix 90° clockwise in-place.
options: A) Transpose then reverse each row
D) Create new matrix O(n^2) space
questions.explanation: Transpose+reverse is simpler; layer rotation also works.

questions.title: Longest Increasing Subsequence (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Length of longest strictly increasing subsequence.
options: B) Patience sorting with binary search O(n log n)
questions.explanation: Patience sorting maintains tails array; binary search to replace.

questions.title: Russian Doll Envelopes (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Maximum number of envelopes you can Russian doll (both dims increasing).
options: A) Sort by width asc, height desc, then LIS on height
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.explanation: Sorting ensures width non-decreasing; LIS on height (strict) ensures both increasing.

questions.title: Maximum Length of Pair Chain (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Longest chain of pairs where second < first of next.
options: A) Sort by second element, greedy chain
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.explanation: Greedy by end yields max chain.

questions.title: Coin Change II (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Number of combinations to make amount (order doesn't matter).
options: A) Outer loop coins, inner loop amount: dp[a] += dp[a-coin]
B) Outer loop amount, inner loop coins gives permutations
C) Both A and B, A gives combinations
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A gives combinations
questions.explanation: Coin-outer order ensures each combination counted once.

questions.title: Partition Equal Subset Sum (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Check if array can be partitioned into two subsets with equal sum.
options: A) 0/1 knapsack DP with target = sum/2
questions.explanation: DP boolean[] dp[j] = dp[j] || dp[j-num]; if sum odd, false.

questions.title: Word Break (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Check if string can be segmented into dictionary words.
options: A) DP: dp[i] = true if dp[j] and s[j:i] in wordDict
questions.explanation: DP O(n^2) or BFS with queue; trie optimizes word lookups.

questions.title: Decode Ways (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count ways to decode digit string (A=1, B=2, ..., Z=26).
questions.explanation: DP bottom-up or top-down with memo.

questions.title: Perfect Squares (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Least number of perfect squares that sum to n.
questions.explanation: DP O(n√n); BFS also works; math says answer ≤4.

questions.title: Unique Paths (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Number of paths from top-left to bottom-right (only right/down).
questions.explanation: Combinatorial formula avoids O(mn) DP.

questions.title: Minimum Path Sum (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum sum path from top-left to bottom-right.
options: B) Dijkstra on grid O(mn log mn)
C) Both A and B, A is simpler
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is simpler
questions.explanation: DP uses DAG property; Dijkstra also works but overkill.

questions.title: Jump Game (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Determine if you can reach last index.
options: A) Greedy: track max reachable index
questions.explanation: Greedy O(n) is optimal; DP O(n^2) works but slower.

questions.title: Jump Game II (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum jumps to reach last index.
options: A) Greedy BFS: jump to farthest reachable within current range
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Track currentEnd, farthest; each iteration, jump++, set currentEnd = farthest.

questions.title: Maximum Subarray Sum with One Deletion (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Maximum subarray sum with at most one deletion.
options: A) Track dp0 (no deletion), dp1 (one deletion)
B) Kadane's variant with skip flag
questions.explanation: dp0[i] = max(dp0[i-1] + nums[i], nums[i]); dp1[i] = max(dp1[i-1] + nums[i], dp0[i-1]).

questions.title: House Robber II (circular, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max sum without robbing adjacent, houses in circle.
options: A) Two cases: rob first not last, rob last not first
B) DP with first excluded and last excluded
questions.explanation: Compute max(rob(nums[0:n-1]), rob(nums[1:n])).

questions.title: Coin Change (minimum coins, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum coins to make amount, return -1 if impossible.
options: B) Greedy fails for some denominations
questions.explanation: DP O(amount * coins) solves unbounded knapsack.

questions.title: Combination Sum IV (permutations, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Number of ways to sum to target (order matters).
options: A) DP: dp[i] = sum(dp[i - num]) for num in nums
B) Outer loop target, inner loop coins (gives permutations)
questions.explanation: Target-outer loop gives permutations; coin-outer gives combinations.

questions.title: Ones and Zeroes (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max number of strings you can form with m zeros and n ones.
options: C) Both A and B, A is optimized
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is optimized
questions.explanation: 2D knapsack DP (reverse loops on zero/one).

questions.title: Minimum Cost For Tickets (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum cost to cover travel days (1-day, 7-day, 30-day passes).
options: A) DP for each day from 1 to 365
B) Use queue for rolling minimum
questions.explanation: dp[i] = min(dp[i-1]+cost[0], dp[i-7]+cost[1], dp[i-30]+cost[2]).

questions.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock II (unlimited, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max profit with unlimited transactions.
options: A) Sum of all positive day-to-day differences
B) Find local mins and maxs and sum difference
questions.explanation: Greedy: profit += max(0, prices[i] - prices[i-1]).

questions.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock III (at most 2, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max profit with at most two transactions.
options: A) DP with states: t1_cost, t1_profit, t2_cost, t2_profit
B) Split array into two and use best time I
questions.explanation: Left to right pass for max profit up to i; right to left for max profit after i; combine.

questions.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock with Cooldown (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max profit with cooldown one day after sell.
options: A) State machine: hold, sell, cooldown
B) DP: cash, hold, cooldown transitions
questions.explanation: hold[i] = max(hold[i-1], cooldown[i-1] - price); cooldown[i] = max(cooldown[i-1], sell[i-1]); sell[i] = hold[i-1] + price.

questions.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock with Transaction Fee (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max profit with transaction fee per trade.
options: B) cash[i] = max(cash[i-1], hold[i-1] + price[i] - fee); hold[i] = max(hold[i-1], cash[i-1] - price[i])
questions.explanation: Standard DP with fee applied on sell.

questions.title: Integer Break (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Break integer into sum of positive integers to maximize product.
options: A) DP: dp[i] = max(j * (i-j), j * dp[i-j])
B) Math: break into 3s if n>4
questions.explanation: Product with 3s is optimal; for 4, 2*2 > 3*1.

questions.title: Ugly Number II (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find nth ugly number (prime factors 2,3,5).
options: A) DP with three pointers (p2, p3, p5)
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: DP: ugly[i] = min(2*ugly[p2], 3*ugly[p3], 5*ugly[p5]); increment the pointer(s) that gave min.

questions.title: Super Ugly Number (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find nth super ugly number for given primes list.
options: A) DP with pointer array per prime
B) Min-heap with set to avoid duplicates
questions.explanation: DP O(n*k) where k is number of primes.

questions.title: Count Numbers with Unique Digits (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count numbers with unique digits in range [0, 10^n).
options: A) DP: f(k) = 9 * P(9, k-1) for k-digit numbers
questions.explanation: For k digits, ways = 9 * 9 * 8 * ... (k-1 factors); sum for k=1..n.

questions.title: Range Sum Query - Immutable (prefix sum, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Query sum of subarray [left, right] multiple times.
options: A) Precompute prefix sum array: sumRange = prefix[right+1] - prefix[left]
B) Compute sum on the fly O(n)
C) Both A and B, A is O(1) per query
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(1) per query
questions.explanation: Prefix sum O(n) preprocess, O(1) query.

questions.title: Range Sum Query 2D - Immutable (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Sum of rectangle (row1,col1) to (row2,col2).
options: A) 2D prefix sum: dp[i][j] = sum of rectangle (0,0) to (i-1,j-1)
C) Both A and B, A is O(1) per query
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(1) per query
questions.explanation: Formula: dp[row2+1][col2+1] - dp[row1][col2+1] - dp[row2+1][col1] + dp[row1][col1].

questions.title: Range Sum Query - Mutable (Fenwick Tree, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Update array and query sum range.
options: A) Fenwick tree (BIT) for O(log n) update and query
D) Prefix sum with update O(n)
questions.explanation: BIT is simpler to implement for point updates and prefix sums.

questions.title: Number of Longest Increasing Subsequence (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count number of longest increasing subsequences.
options: A) DP storing length and count for each position
B) Segment tree for range max length and count
D) LIS length then counting paths
questions.explanation: DP O(n^2): for each i, find all j with nums[j] < nums[i], combine lengths and counts.

questions.title: Count of Smaller Numbers After Self (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: For each element, count smaller elements to its right.
options: B) Merge sort counting inversions from right
C) Fenwick tree (BIT) with coordinate compression
questions.explanation: Merge sort variant: merge in reverse order to count; BIT also works.

questions.title: Reverse Pairs (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count pairs (i,j) with i<j and nums[i] > 2*nums[j].
options: A) Merge sort with two-pointer merge step
B) Fenwick tree after coordinate compression
questions.explanation: Merge sort: during merge, count pairs across halves.

questions.title: Number of Subarrays with Bounded Maximum (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count subarrays where max element between L and R.
options: A) Count subarrays with max <= R minus count with max < L
B) Sliding window with last invalid index
questions.explanation: Helper function count(max ≤ bound): sliding window, add window length when bound not exceeded.

questions.title: Subarrays with K Different Integers (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count subarrays with exactly K distinct integers.
options: B) Sliding window with hash map and shrink
questions.explanation: atMost(K) counts subarrays with ≤ K distinct; exactly K = atMost(K) - atMost(K-1).

questions.title: Longest Continuous Subarray With Absolute Diff ≤ Limit (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Longest subarray where max-min <= limit.
options: A) Two deques for max and min
B) Balanced BST O(n log n)
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Deques maintain indices of max and min; while diff > limit, increment left and update deques.

questions.title: Minimum Window Substring (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum window in s containing all chars of t.
options: A) Expand right until all chars found, then shrink left
B) Two pointers with frequency map and formed count
questions.explanation: Standard O(n) sliding window with required counter.

questions.title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Replace at most k chars to make longest repeating substring.
options: A) Expand window, maintain max frequency, shrink if (length - maxFreq) > k
B) Binary search on answer O(n log n)
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Sliding window ensures window size - max count <= k; expand right, shrink left when needed.

questions.title: Fruit Into Baskets (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max number of fruits in two baskets (at most 2 types).
options: A) Longest substring with at most 2 distinct characters
B) Sliding window with hash map
questions.explanation: Sliding window: when third type appears, shrink until only 2 types remain.

questions.title: Max Consecutive Ones III (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max consecutive 1's after flipping at most k zeros.
options: A) Sliding window: count zeros, when zeros > k shrink
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Window expands right; if zeros > k, move left; track max window size.

questions.title: String Compression (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Compress string using run-length encoding in-place.
options: A) Two pointers: read, write, count consecutive chars
B) Build result string then copy back
questions.explanation: Traverse, count same chars, write char and count (if >1).

questions.title: Product of Array Except Self (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Return product of all elements except self without division.
options: A) Prefix and suffix product arrays
B) Use one array for prefix, multiply suffix on fly
C) Both A and B O(n) time O(1) extra space (except output)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B O(n) time O(1) extra space (except output)
questions.explanation: Left pass: output[i] = product of left; right pass: multiply by product of right.

questions.title: Increasing Triplet Subsequence (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Return true if there exists increasing triplet.
options: A) Greedy: track first and second smallest
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Keep first and second; if current > second, true; else if current < first, update first; else if current > first and < second, update second.

questions.title: Shortest Unsorted Continuous Subarray (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Length of smallest subarray that sorting makes whole array sorted.
options: A) Compare with sorted array O(n log n)
B) Find left boundary where nums[i] > minRight, right boundary where nums[i] < maxLeft
C) Both A and B, B is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, B is O(n)
questions.explanation: Left pass: track max from left, find last index where nums[i] < max; right pass: track min from right, find first index where nums[i] > min.

questions.title: Find All Numbers Disappeared in Array (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Return missing numbers in array of 1..n.
options: A) Mark presence by negating index
D) Both A and marking O(n) time O(1) space
questions.correct_answer: D) Both A and marking O(n) time O(1) space
questions.explanation: For each num, mark nums[abs(num)-1] negative; then indices with positive are missing.

questions.title: Move Zeroes (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Move all zeros to the end preserving order.
options: A) Two-pointer: swap non-zero with first zero
questions.explanation: Pointer lastNonZero = 0; for i in range(n): if nums[i] != 0: swap and lastNonZero++.

questions.title: Maximum Product of Word Lengths (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max product of lengths of two words with no common letters.
options: A) Compare all pairs O(n^2 * L)
B) Convert each word to bitmask (int), then (mask[i] & mask[j]) == 0
C) Both A and B, B is faster
D) Group by mask then max product
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, B is faster
questions.explanation: Bitmask for 26 letters; if no common letters, product is candidate.

questions.title: Subsets II (with duplicates, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Return all unique subsets (with duplicate elements).
options: A) Sort, backtrack with start index, skip duplicates
B) Use set to store subsets
C) Both A and B, A is efficient
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is efficient
questions.explanation: Sort array; in backtracking, if i > start and nums[i] == nums[i-1], skip.

questions.title: Combination Sum II (no reuse, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find unique combinations that sum to target (each number used once).
options: A) Sort, backtrack with start=i+1, skip duplicates
C) Both A and B, A is efficient
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is efficient
questions.explanation: Sort ascending; in recursion, skip duplicates by checking i > start and nums[i]==nums[i-1].

questions.title: Palindrome Partitioning (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Partition string such that each substring is palindrome.
options: A) Backtracking with palindrome check O(n*2^n)
B) Precompute palindrome DP table, then backtrack O(1) per check
questions.explanation: Precompute isPalindrome[i][j] for O(1) checks during backtracking.

questions.title: N-Queens (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Place n queens on n×n board without attacking.
options: A) Backtracking row by row, track cols, diag1, diag2
B) Bitmask optimization for columns and diagonals
questions.explanation: Use sets or arrays for columns, positive diag (i-j), negative diag (i+j); bitmask faster.

questions.title: Sudoku Solver (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Solve given Sudoku puzzle.
options: A) Backtracking with constraint checking (row, col, box)
questions.explanation: Standard backtracking works for 9x9; Dancing Links is for exact cover.

questions.title: Valid Sudoku (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Determine if 9x9 Sudoku board is valid (no need to solve).
options: A) Three arrays: rows[9][9], cols[9][9], boxes[3][3][9]
B) Use hash set for each row/col/box
questions.explanation: Box index = (i/3)*3 + j/3; mark seen numbers.

questions.title: Word Ladder (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Length of shortest transformation sequence from beginWord to endWord.
options: A) BFS by generating all next words (26*L each)
questions.explanation: Bidirectional BFS halves search space.

questions.title: Minimum Genetic Mutation (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Min mutations from start to end gene (A,C,G,T).
options: A) BFS, generate next by changing one char
questions.explanation: Similar to word ladder but with 4 letters and bank.

questions.title: Open the Lock (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Min turns to reach target from '0000' avoiding deadends.
options: A) BFS on 10000 states, each with 8 moves
questions.explanation: Bidirectional BFS reduces states visited.

questions.title: Rotting Oranges (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Time to rot all oranges, or -1 if impossible.
options: A) Multi-source BFS from all initially rotten oranges
questions.explanation: Multi-source BFS: queue all rotten, propagate level by level.

questions.title: Shortest Path in Binary Matrix (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Shortest clear path from (0,0) to (n-1,n-1) (8 directions).
questions.explanation: BFS ensures shortest path in unweighted grid.

questions.title: Is Graph Bipartite? (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Check if graph is bipartite.
options: A) BFS coloring with two colors
questions.explanation: BFS: assign color 0, neighbors get 1; if neighbor has same color, false.

questions.title: Possible Bipartition (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Given dislikes pairs, can partition into two groups?
options: A) Same as bipartite graph detection
questions.explanation: Build graph from dislikes, check bipartite.

questions.title: Course Schedule II (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Return ordering of courses if possible.
questions.explanation: Kahn's: queue nodes with indegree 0, add to result, decrement indegrees of neighbors.

questions.title: Alien Dictionary (Tech Mahindra, concept)
questions.problem_statement: Find order of letters from sorted alien words.
options: A) Compare adjacent words to build graph, then topological sort
questions.explanation: If cycle, invalid; else return topological order.

questions.title: Reconstruct Itinerary (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Reconstruct itinerary using all tickets once (lexicographically smallest).
options: A) Hierholzer's algorithm with priority queue
questions.explanation: DFS with adjacency lists sorted; post-order yields reverse path.

questions.title: Network Delay Time (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Time to broadcast from node k to all nodes.
questions.explanation: Dijkstra O(E log V) for positive weights.

questions.title: Cheapest Flights Within K Stops (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Cheapest price with at most K stops.
options: A) Modified Dijkstra with (node, stops) state
questions.explanation: Bellman-Ford: dp[k][v] = min(dp[k-1][v], dp[k-1][u] + cost).

questions.title: Redundant Connection (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find edge to remove to make tree.
options: A) Union-find: if find(u)==find(v), edge redundant
questions.explanation: Union-find: for each edge, if roots same, return edge; else union.

questions.title: Number of Provinces (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count number of connected components (friend circles).
options: A) DFS/BFS from each unvisited node
questions.explanation: Union-find: for each i,j if isConnected[i][j], union(i,j); count distinct roots.

questions.title: Accounts Merge (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Merge accounts with common emails.
options: A) Build graph of emails, DFS components
B) Union-find on emails, map email->account index
D) Sort by name then email
questions.explanation: Union-find on email indices; then group by root.

questions.title: Evaluate Division (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Evaluate queries a/b = value.
options: A) Build weighted graph, DFS each query
B) Union-find with weight ratio to root
questions.explanation: Union-find: each node stores ratio to parent; find returns root and scaling factor.

questions.title: Satisfiability of Equality Equations (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Check if equations like a==b and a!=b are satisfiable.
options: A) Union all == equations, then check != for same parent
B) Build graph of equality, check inequality in same component
questions.explanation: Union-find for equality; after union, for each !=, check if both in same component -> false.

questions.title: Keys and Rooms (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Can we visit all rooms starting from room 0?
options: A) BFS/DFS from room 0 using keys found
B) Union-find on rooms sharing keys
questions.explanation: BFS: maintain visited set; use keys to unlock new rooms.

questions.title: Shortest Bridge (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum flips to connect two islands.
options: A) Find first island via DFS, then BFS from its boundary
B) Multi-source BFS from all 1s with distance tracking
questions.explanation: DFS to mark first island; BFS from its perimeter to find second island.

questions.title: Max Area of Island (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find maximum area of an island.
options: A) DFS each '1', compute area, track max
questions.explanation: Iterate grid, for each unvisited '1', run DFS/BFS to calculate size.

questions.title: Surrounded Regions (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Flip all 'O's that are not on boundary and not connected to boundary to 'X'.
options: A) Start from boundary 'O's, DFS/BFS to mark safe 'O's, then flip others
B) Union-find with boundary dummy node
questions.explanation: Mark all 'O's connected to boundary; then flip unmarked 'O's to 'X'.

questions.title: Number of Enclaves (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count number of land cells that cannot reach boundary.
options: A) DFS/BFS from boundary '1's to mark reachable, then count unmarked
questions.explanation: Similar to surrounded regions but for 1's; count cells not visited.

questions.title: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find cells where water can flow to both Pacific and Atlantic.
options: A) Multi-source BFS from Pacific and Atlantic borders
questions.explanation: Start from ocean borders and flow inward (higher or equal). Intersection of reachable sets.

questions.title: Longest Increasing Path in Matrix (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Longest strictly increasing path in matrix.
options: A) DFS from each cell with memoization
B) Topological sort on directed edges
questions.explanation: DFS + memo: longest path from cell = 1 + max(neighbors with higher value).

metadata.note: Questions 151-300. No overlap with previous Infosys, Microsoft, or first 150 Tech Mahindra questions. Focus on practical problem-solving and optimization.

questions.title: Minimum Falling Path Sum (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum sum of falling path from top to bottom (can move down, down-left, down-right).
options: A) DP[i][j] = matrix[i][j] + min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i-1][j-1], dp[i-1][j+1])
C) Both A and B, A is O(n^2)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n^2)
questions.explanation: Bottom-up DP, handle boundary columns separately.

questions.title: Triangle (Minimum Path Sum, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum path sum from top to bottom in triangle array.
options: A) Bottom-up DP: dp[j] = triangle[i][j] + min(dp[j], dp[j+1])
questions.explanation: Bottom-up in-place DP using O(n) space.

questions.title: Minimum Cost to Merge Stones (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum cost to merge all stones into one pile (merge k consecutive piles).
options: C) Both A and DP O(n^3)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and DP O(n^3)
questions.explanation: DP[i][j] = min(dp[i][mid] + dp[mid+1][j]) + sum(i,j) for valid mid.

questions.title: Minimum Cost to Make at Least One Valid Path in Grid (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum changes to arrow directions to reach bottom-right.
options: A) 0-1 BFS (deque) with cost 0 if follow arrow, 1 otherwise
C) Both A and B, A is O(m*n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(m*n)
questions.explanation: Treat grid as graph with edges cost 0 along arrow direction, cost 1 to change.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Refueling Stops (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum refueling stops to reach target.
options: A) Max-heap of fuel from passed stations, take max when out of fuel
B) DP: dp[t] = max distance with t stops
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.explanation: Simulate: add all reachable stations to max-heap; when fuel insufficient, pop largest.

questions.title: Minimum Difficulty of a Job Schedule (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Partition array into d days minimizing sum of daily maxes.
options: A) DP[i][d] = min over j of (dp[j][d-1] + max(j+1..i))
questions.explanation: O(n^2*d) DP can be optimized to O(n*d) using monotonic stack.

questions.title: Maximum Performance of a Team (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max performance = sum(speed) * min(efficiency), team size ≤ k.
options: A) Sort by efficiency descending, maintain min-heap of speeds
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.explanation: Iterate engineers sorted by efficiency, add speed to heap, if size>k pop smallest.

questions.title: Maximum Profit in Job Scheduling (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max profit from non-overlapping jobs.
options: A) Sort by end, DP with binary search for last non-conflict
questions.explanation: Weighted interval scheduling O(n log n).

questions.title: Minimum Cost to Hire K Workers (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Hire k workers, each paid wage ≥ quality * ratio, min total cost.
options: A) Sort by wage/quality ratio, maintain max-heap of qualities
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.explanation: For each worker as captain, ratio = wage/quality; maintain max-heap of qualities; if size>k pop largest, answer = ratio * sum(qualities).

questions.title: Divide Array in Sets of K Consecutive Numbers (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Partition array into groups of k consecutive numbers.
options: A) Count frequencies, for each smallest number try to form group
questions.explanation: TreeMap of frequencies; while map not empty, take smallest, decrement next k numbers.

questions.title: Hand of Straights (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Check if can rearrange into groups of consecutive numbers of size W.
options: A) Same as divide array in sets
questions.explanation: Exactly same as LeetCode 846.

questions.title: Least Number of Unique Integers after K Removals (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Remove k elements to minimize number of unique integers.
options: A) Count frequencies, sort by frequency ascending, remove smallest frequencies first
B) Use heap to remove smallest frequency
questions.explanation: Greedy: remove elements with least frequency first.

questions.title: Delete and Earn (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max points by deleting numbers, if delete x then cannot delete x-1 or x+1.
options: A) Transform to house robber: points[i] = sum of all num i
questions.explanation: Count frequency; DP over value range: take or skip.

questions.title: Maximum Sum of 3 Non-Overlapping Subarrays (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max sum of three length-k subarrays that are non-overlapping.
options: A) Precompute window sums, leftBest, rightBest, iterate middle
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Sliding window sums; left[i] = best index of one subarray up to i; right[i] from right.

questions.title: Frog Jump (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Frog jumps from stone to stone with step difference k-1, k, k+1.
options: A) DP: map[stone] = set of jump lengths possible
questions.explanation: For each stone, for each jump in set, try jump-1, jump, jump+1 to next stone.

questions.title: Sum of Distances in Tree (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Sum of distances from each node to all others.
options: A) First DFS compute count and sum from root, second DFS reroot formula
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
D) BFS from each node O(n^2)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: dp[node] = dp[parent] - count[node] + (n - count[node]).

questions.title: Count Submatrices With All Ones (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count submatrices with all 1's.
options: A) For each row, treat as histogram, count using stack
B) Prefix sum and binary search fails
C) Both A and DP with stack O(n^2)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and DP with stack O(n^2)
questions.explanation: For each row, compute heights (consecutive 1's), use stack to count submatrices ending at each cell.

questions.title: Minimum Cost Tree From Leaf Values (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum cost of tree where cost = product of adjacent leaves, combine smallest.
options: A) Monotonic decreasing stack: pop smaller and combine with adjacent min
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Use stack; when arr[i] >= stack.top(), pop and add product with min(stack.pop(), arr[i]).

questions.title: Shortest Palindrome (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Add characters to front to make palindrome, shortest result.
options: B) KMP: compute LPS for s + '#' + reverse(s)
questions.explanation: Find longest palindromic prefix; answer = reverse(suffix) + s.

questions.title: Palindrome Pairs (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find all pairs of words that concatenate to palindrome.
options: A) For each word, check all others O(n^2 * L)
B) Build Trie of reversed words, check prefixes
C) Use hash map of reversed words
questions.explanation: Map approach: for each word, check if reverse exists; also check prefixes/suffixes that are palindromes.

questions.title: Valid Palindrome III (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Can string become palindrome after removing at most k chars?
options: A) DP: longest palindromic subsequence length, check if n - lps <= k
questions.explanation: LPS length via DP; if n - lps_length <= k, true.

questions.title: Count Different Palindromic Subsequences (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count distinct palindromic subsequences in string.
options: A) DP with 3D: dp[i][j][char] but careful
B) Divide and conquer with set
questions.explanation: DP[i][j] = set of distinct palindromic subsequences; combine using earliest and latest occurrence.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Events That Can Be Attended II (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Attend at most k events, maximize profit.
options: A) Sort by end, DP[i][k] = max(DP[i-1][k], profit[i] + DP[p][k-1])
questions.explanation: Weighted interval scheduling with K events.

questions.title: Maximum Height by Stacking Cuboids (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Stack cuboids (can rotate) with non-decreasing dimensions, max total height.
options: A) Sort each cuboid dimensions, sort all cuboids, LIS on width and depth
questions.explanation: Sort each cuboid's dimensions; sort all cuboids; DP: dp[i] = height[i] + max(dp[j]) where j can be placed under i.

questions.title: Minimum Cost to Make Array Continuous (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Make array continuous (max-min = n-1) with min changes.
options: A) Sort unique values, sliding window of length n
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.explanation: Remove duplicates, sort; sliding window: longest window with difference ≤ n-1; answer = n - maxWindow.

questions.title: Minimum Increment to Make Array Unique (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum increments to make array elements unique.
options: A) Sort, track nextExpected; if curr < nextExpected, increment
B) Use count array for freq
questions.explanation: After sorting, maintain prev; if nums[i] <= prev, moves += prev - nums[i] + 1; prev++.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Moves to Make Palindrome (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum adjacent swaps to make string palindrome.
options: A) Two pointers: if mismatch, find matching char from right, swap step by step
questions.explanation: Greedy: fix leftmost, find matching from right, bring it by swapping.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Operations to Move All Balls to Each Box (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: For each box, min moves to move all balls to that box.
options: A) Two passes: left to right accumulate moves, right to left, sum
B) For each ball, add distance O(n^2)
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
D) Use BFS from each box
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Left pass: count balls to left, moves = leftCount + leftMoves; right pass similar.

questions.title: Minimum Swaps to Group All 1's Together (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum swaps to group all 1's together (adjacent).
options: A) Window of size = total ones, find max ones in window, answer = totalOnes - maxOnes
B) Two pointers with zero counts
questions.explanation: Sliding window of length k (# of ones), track max ones, swaps = k - maxOnes.

questions.title: Minimum Adjacent Swaps for K Consecutive Ones (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum swaps to make k consecutive 1's.
options: A) Get indices of 1's, use prefix sum, window size k, cost = sum distance to median
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Collect indices of 1's; for window of k indices, median minimizes sum distance; compute using prefix sums.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Frogs Croaking (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum number of frogs to make 'croak' sounds without overlapping.
options: A) Track counts of 'c','r','o','a','k', if invalid return -1, answer = max count of 'c' in progress
questions.explanation: Maintain counters; when 'c' increment, when 'k' decrement; track max concurrent croaks.

questions.title: Largest Component Size by Common Factor (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Largest component size where two numbers connected if share factor >1.
options: A) Union each number with its prime factors
B) Build graph of numbers, edges for gcd>1 O(n^2)
C) Both A and B, A is O(n sqrt(max))
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n sqrt(max))
questions.explanation: For each number, find prime factors, union number with each prime factor (treat primes as nodes).

questions.title: Number of Submatrices That Sum to Target (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count submatrices with sum equal to target.
options: A) Fix top and bottom rows, reduce to 1D subarray sum equals target
C) Both A and B, A is O(n^3)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n^3)
questions.explanation: For each top row, compute prefix row sums, then for each bottom row, use hash map of prefix sums.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Events That Can Be Attended (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max number of events you can attend (each event can be attended only on one day).
options: A) Sort events by start, use min-heap of end times
B) Greedy: attend event with earliest end first
questions.explanation: For each day, add events starting today to heap; attend event with earliest end.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Taps to Open to Water a Garden (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum taps to cover [0,n], each tap covers [i-r, i+r].
options: A) Convert to interval covering, sort by start, greedy take farthest reach
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.explanation: For each position, compute max right reachable; greedy jumps.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Visible Points (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max points visible from location with angle ≤ angle, considering points exactly at location.
options: A) Convert points to angles, sort, double array, sliding window of size angle
questions.explanation: Compute angle using atan2, sort, duplicate with +2π, two pointers for max within angle.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Groups Getting Fresh Donuts (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Group batches to maximize happy groups (sum % batchSize == 0).
options: A) DP on count of remainders, state is vector of remainder counts
B) Greedy pair remainders that sum to batchSize
C) Both A and B, A is exact
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is exact
questions.explanation: DP state: count of each remainder; transition by picking a remainder group.

questions.title: Count Ways to Build Rooms in an Ant Colony (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Number of ways to build rooms in tree order (parent before children).
options: A) DP with combination: dp[node] = product(dp[child]) * (size-1)! / product(size[child]!)
questions.explanation: Multinomial coefficient: ways = (total children)! / product(childSize!) * product(dp[child]).

questions.title: Maximum Number of Groups with Increasing Length (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Maximum number of groups you can form with strictly increasing lengths.
options: A) Sort, greedy: start with size 1, increment while cumulative sum <= total
B) Binary search on group count
questions.explanation: Greedy: smallest groups first; require sum of 1..k ≤ n.

questions.title: Minimum Time to Make Rope Colorful (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Remove balloons to avoid consecutive same color, cost = neededTime[i]; min total cost.
options: A) For each group of same color, keep max cost, remove rest
B) Two pointers: sum all, subtract max each group
questions.explanation: Scan, when colors[i]==colors[i-1], add min(time[i-1], time[i]). Actually keep max, remove others.

questions.title: Minimum Time to Repair Cars (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum time to repair all cars given ranks, time = rank * n^2.
options: A) Binary search on time, check function: sum floor(sqrt(time/rank)) >= cars
B) Greedy assign smallest rank first fails
questions.explanation: Binary search on T; for each rank, compute cars fixed = floor(sqrt(T/rank)).

questions.title: Minimum Time to Complete Trips (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum time to complete at least totalTrips given bus trip times.
options: A) Binary search on time, check trips = sum(time / busTime[i]) >= totalTrips
B) Use priority queue simulation O(totalTrips log n)
questions.explanation: Binary search on T; each bus completes floor(T/time[i]) trips.

questions.title: Number of Ways to Rearrange Sticks With K Sticks Visible (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Number of permutations of n sticks where exactly k are visible from left.
questions.explanation: DP: new stick either placed at front (visible count+1) or after any of i-1 sticks (visible same).

questions.title: Painting the Walls (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Min cost to paint walls, each painter can paint 1 wall in time, paid per time, or hire paid worker.
options: A) DP dp[i][j] = min cost to paint i walls with j paid time
B) Greedy by cost/time ratio fails
questions.explanation: DP: for each wall, choose paid (add cost, add time) or free (cost, time).

questions.title: Number of Flowers in Full Bloom (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: For each person at time t, count flowers blooming.
options: A) Sweep line + prefix sums, binary search for each person
B) For each person, iterate flowers O(n*m)
C) Both A and B, A is O((n+m) log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O((n+m) log n)
questions.explanation: Collect start and end events; sort; for each person binary search start and end count.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Robots Within Budget (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max number of consecutive robots such that cost = max(charge) + sum(run) * k ≤ budget.
options: A) Sliding window, maintain max deque for charge, prefix sum for run
B) Binary search on length, check condition O(n log n)
questions.explanation: Two pointers: maintain max (deque) and sum (prefix); expand right, shrink left if cost>budget.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Consecutive Values You Can Make (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Maximum number of consecutive integers you can form from coins.
options: A) Sort coins, maintain maxReach = 1; for coin, if coin > maxReach break; else maxReach += coin
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.explanation: If next coin > current reach+1, cannot make current reach+1.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Eaten Apples (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max apples eaten given daily apples and rot days.
options: A) Min-heap of (rot day, count), simulate day by day
B) Greedy eat best apple fails
questions.explanation: Priority queue (min-heap) on expiration day; each day pop expired, push new apples, eat one.

questions.title: Minimum Moves to Equal Array Elements (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum increments to make all equal, operation: increment n-1 elements by 1.
questions.explanation: Equivalent to decrementing one element each move, so moves = sum - n*min.

questions.title: Minimum Moves to Equal Array Elements II (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum moves to make all equal by increment/decrement any element.
options: B) Use nth_element to find median O(n)
questions.explanation: Median minimizes sum of absolute deviations.

questions.title: Minimum Moves to Make Array Complementary (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Make pairs sum equal to target, min changes.
options: A) For each pair (a,b), compute ranges for target; use difference array to track cost
D) Greedy choose target as median
questions.explanation: For each pair, min changes needed for target is 0,1,2 depending on range; use sweep line.

questions.title: Minimum Changes to Make Alternating Binary String (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum flips to make string alternating (0101... or 1010...).
options: A) Compare with two patterns, return min of mismatches
B) Greedy flip based on index parity
questions.explanation: Prefix parity: cost1 = count where s[i] != (i%2==0?'0':'1'), cost2 = n - cost1.

questions.title: Number of Ways to Split Array Into Two Subarrays with Equal Sum (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Number of splits where sum left >= sum right.
options: A) Prefix sum, total, count where prefix >= total-prefix
questions.explanation: Simple O(n) prefix sum and count condition.

questions.title: Maximum AND Sum of Array After Pairing (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Split array into pairs, each pair contributes AND, maximize sum.
options: A) DP[mask] = max over i,j not used of dp[mask|(1<<i)|(1<<j)] + (nums[i]&nums[j])
B) Greedy sort by AND fails
questions.explanation: Bitmask DP O(2^n * n^2) where n≤16, feasible.

questions.title: Maximum Number of People That Can Be Caught in Tag (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Each police can catch nearest robber within distance k, maximize caught.
options: A) Two pointers: police and robber, move pointers greedily
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Sort positions, use two pointers to match each police to nearest uncaught robber within distance.

questions.title: Maximum Product of the Length of Two Palindromic Subsequences (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max product of lengths of two disjoint palindromic subsequences.
options: A) Enumerate all subsequences via bitmask, check palindrome, track best pair with disjoint masks
C) Both A and B, A is O(2^n * n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(2^n * n)
questions.explanation: n ≤ 12, so 2^n = 4096 feasible; check palindrome in O(n), iterate over masks.

questions.title: Minimum Operations to Make a Subsequence (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum operations to make target a subsequence of arr by inserting any number.
options: A) Map target values to indices, convert arr to sequence of indices, find LIS
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n) if no duplicates
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n) if no duplicates
questions.explanation: If target has unique elements, map value -> index; for arr, keep indices; LIS length = longest common subsequence.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Removals to Make Mountain Array (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Min removals to make array mountain (strictly increase then decrease).
options: A) Compute LIS from left and LDS from right, mountain at i = leftLIS[i] + rightLDS[i] -1, answer = n - maxMountain
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n)
questions.explanation: LIS from left, LDS from right (actually LIS on reversed), then max of left+right-1.

questions.title: Count Pairs With XOR in a Range (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count pairs (i<j) with xor between low and high.
options: A) Trie with count of numbers, query countLess(high) - countLess(low-1)
C) Both A and B, A is O(n * bitLength)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n * bitLength)
questions.explanation: Build binary trie; for each number, count numbers with xor ≤ limit using trie traversal.

questions.title: Check If a String Contains All Binary Codes of Size K (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Check if all binary strings of length k appear in s.
options: A) Generate all 2^k patterns, check substring O(2^k * n)
B) Rolling hash with set (sliding window), count unique substrings of length k
C) Both A and B, B is O(n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, B is O(n)
questions.explanation: Sliding window to add all substrings of length k to set; if set size == 2^k return true.

questions.title: Maximum Strictly Increasing Cells in a Matrix (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Max path length where each step moves to larger value in same row or column.
options: A) Sort cells by value, update rowMax and colMax, answer = max(rowMax[i], colMax[j]) + 1
B) DFS from each cell O(mn)
C) Both A and B, A is O(mn log mn)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(mn log mn)
questions.explanation: Process cells in increasing order; dp[i][j] = max(rowMax[i], colMax[j]) + 1; then update rowMax[i] and colMax[j].

questions.title: Number of Ways to Stay in the Same Place After Some Steps (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Number of ways to stay at index 0 after exactly steps steps, moving left/right/stay.
options: B) 1D DP optimized, pos ≤ min(step, arrLen-1)
questions.explanation: DP O(steps * min(steps, arrLen)), space optimized to 2 rows.

questions.title: Minimum Skips to Arrive at Meeting On Time (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Min skips of rest to arrive within hours.
options: A) DP[i][k] = min time to reach i with k skips, use rational numbers
B) Binary search on skips + check DP
questions.explanation: DP O(n^2) using integer arithmetic: time = ceil(prevTime + dist/speed) or not ceil if skip.

questions.title: String Compression II (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Run-length encode after deleting up to k chars, min compressed length.
options: A) DP[i][k] = min over j of (dp[j][k - (i-j - sameCount)] + compressedLen(s[j+1..i]))
questions.explanation: DP O(n^2 * k) with preprocessing of same chars in range.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Work Sessions to Finish Tasks (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Min sessions to complete tasks, each session at most sessionTime.
options: A) DP[mask] = min sessions, iterate submask to fill session
questions.explanation: DP O(3^n) using submask enumeration or O(2^n * n) with recursive filling.

questions.title: Minimum Time to Collect All Apples in a Tree (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Min time to collect all apples starting at root 0.
options: A) DFS: if subtree has apple, add 2 for edge (go and back)
questions.explanation: Post-order DFS: if child subtree has apple or child itself has apple, add 2 to time.

questions.title: Maximum Number of Tasks You Can Assign (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Assign tasks to workers with strength, can take pill to boost strength by k.
options: A) Binary search on task count, greedy assign hardest task to weakest sufficient worker
C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n log n)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n log n log n)
questions.explanation: Check mid: assign largest mid tasks, use multiset for workers.

questions.title: Minimum Suffix Flips (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum suffix flips to turn binary string into target.
options: A) Greedy: flip when current bit != target bit, count flips
questions.explanation: Track current flipped state; if (flipped xor s[i]) != target[i], increment count and toggle flipped.

questions.title: Minimum Number of Increments on Subarrays to Form a Target Array (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum operations to transform zero array to target by incrementing contiguous subarray by 1.
options: A) Answer = sum(max(0, target[i] - target[i-1])) for i from 0 to n
B) Use monotonic stack to count increments
questions.explanation: Each increase from previous value requires that many new operations starting at i.

questions.title: Minimum Cost to Connect All Points (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Manhattan distance MST min cost.
options: A) Prim's with O(n^2) using Manhattan
B) Kruskal with edges sorted O(n^2 log n)
C) Both A and B, A is O(n^2)
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n^2)
questions.explanation: Prim's on complete graph of n points with Manhattan distance runs in O(n^2).

questions.title: Minimum Number of Days to Disconnect Island (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum days to disconnect island by removing land cells.
options: A) If already disconnected, return 0; else if any articulation point, return 1; else return 2
B) BFS count components after removing each cell O(n^2 * m^2)
C) Both A and B, A is efficient
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is efficient
questions.explanation: Check connected components; if >1 return 0. Find articulation points using Tarjan.

questions.title: Find All People With Secret (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Given meetings at times, and first person with secret, return all who know secret.
options: A) Sort meetings by time, union people who attend same meeting with secret holder
B) BFS over time with disjoint set
questions.explanation: Process meetings per time bucket; union all attendees, then for each component if any know secret, all know.

questions.title: Minimum Time to Visit a Cell In a Grid (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimum time to reach bottom-right where time ≥ grid value.
options: A) BFS with priority queue (Dijkstra) with waiting parity adjustment
B) DP with time dimension O(m*n*maxTime)
questions.explanation: Dijkstra: if currTime < grid[nx][ny], wait until time = grid[nx][ny] if parity matches, else +1.

questions.title: Design Twitter (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Design Twitter with postTweet, getNewsFeed, follow, unfollow.
options: A) Store tweets in list per user, feed = merge k lists with heap
B) Use global tweet list with timestamps, filter followees
C) Both A and B, A is common
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is common
questions.explanation: For each user, store own tweets. Feed: get recent tweets from user and followed users, use max-heap by time.

questions.title: Design HashMap (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Design hash map without built-in libraries.
options: A) Use array of buckets (linked list for collision) size 10^4
B) Use linear probing open addressing
D) Use binary search tree per bucket
questions.explanation: Chaining with linked list or array of nodes is most common.

questions.title: Design HashSet (Tech Mahindra)
