questions.problem_statement: Design hash set without built-in libraries.
options: A) Boolean array of size 10^6 (space heavy)
B) Array of buckets with linked list
C) Both A and B, B is scalable
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, B is scalable
questions.explanation: If key range known and moderate, boolean array works; otherwise chaining.

questions.title: Design Circular Deque (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Design circular double-ended queue with fixed size.
options: A) Use array with front and rear pointers
C) Both A and B, A is space-efficient
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is space-efficient
questions.explanation: Circular array with modulo indexing: front and rear pointers, handle full/empty.

questions.title: Design Circular Queue (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Design circular queue with enQueue, deQueue, Front, Rear, isFull, isEmpty.
options: A) Use array with head and tail pointers, size counter
C) Both A and B, array is typical
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, array is typical
questions.explanation: Circular buffer: keep one slot empty or maintain count.

questions.title: Encode and Decode TinyURL (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Design URL shortening service.
options: B) Random fixed-length string with collision check
questions.explanation: Store mapping in DB or map; encode ID to short key (base62).

questions.title: Shuffle an Array (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Shuffle array in-place with equal probability.
options: A) Generate random index and swap with current index (Fisher-Yates)
B) Use sort with random comparator (biased)
C) Both A and B but A is uniform
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B but A is uniform
questions.explanation: Fisher-Yates: for i from n-1 to 1, swap with random index 0..i.

questions.title: Random Pick Index (Reservoir Sampling, Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Pick random index of target in array (multiple occurrences) with equal probability.
options: A) Collect all indices and pick random O(n)
B) Reservoir sampling: count occurrences, pick with probability 1/count
C) Both A and B, B works for streaming
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, B works for streaming
questions.explanation: Reservoir: keep result, count=1; for next matching index, replace result with probability 1/count.

questions.title: Insert Delete GetRandom O(1) (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Data structure supporting insert, delete, getRandom in O(1).
options: A) HashMap + ArrayList: map element->index, list stores elements
C) Both A and B, A is standard
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is standard
questions.explanation: Delete: swap with last element, update map, pop from list.

questions.title: Design Underground System (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Track check-in/check-out with average travel time.
options: A) Two maps: checkIns (id -> (station, time)), averages (start+end -> (sum, count))
B) One map storing trips, compute on the fly
C) Both A and B, A is efficient
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is efficient
questions.explanation: On check-in, store in map; on check-out, lookup check-in, update aggregates.

questions.title: Time Based Key-Value Store (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Store multiple values per key with timestamps, get value at given timestamp.
options: A) HashMap mapping key to list of (timestamp, value) pairs, binary search
B) TreeMap per key for floorKey
D) Use array of all timestamps
questions.explanation: Sorted list per key; get: binary search to find largest timestamp ≤ given stamp.

questions.title: Snapshot Array (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Array that supports snap() and get(index, snap_id).
options: A) For each index, list of (snap_id, value), binary search on snap_id
B) Store full array per snapshot (space heavy)
C) Both A and B, A is space-efficient
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is space-efficient
questions.explanation: Each index maintains a tree map or list; when set, add (snap_id, value).

questions.title: Number of Matching Subsequences (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count strings in array that are subsequences of s.
options: A) For each word, check subsequence O(s.length * words.length)
B) Preprocess s: array of lists for each char (indices), binary search for each word
C) Both A and B, B is faster
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, B is faster
questions.explanation: Store for each character the list of positions; for each word, binary search to find next occurrence.

questions.title: Short Encoding of Words (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Minimal length of reference string that encodes list of words (each word ends with #).
options: A) Insert reversed words into trie, answer = sum(node depth +1) for leaf nodes
B) Sort by length descending, remove words that are suffix of previous
D) Use hash set of suffixes
questions.explanation: Trie of reversed words: leaf nodes represent words not suffix of another, sum lengths + #.

questions.title: Replace Words (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Replace all words in sentence with their shortest root from dictionary.
options: A) Trie of roots, for each word find shortest prefix match
B) For each word, check all roots O(n*m)
C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
D) Sort roots and binary search
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(n)
questions.explanation: Build trie of roots; for each word, traverse trie until end (if prefix found) else keep original.

questions.title: Map Sum Pairs (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Insert key-value pairs, sum of values for keys starting with given prefix.
options: A) Trie with node storing sum of all values in subtree
B) HashMap storing key->value, for prefix sum iterate over keys O(n)
C) Both A and B, A is O(L) per operation
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is O(L) per operation
questions.explanation: Trie: each node keeps cumulative sum; on insert, update all nodes in path.

questions.title: Implement Stack using Queues (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Implement stack using only two queues.
options: A) Make push O(1), pop O(n) by rotating queue
B) Make push O(n), pop O(1) by moving elements
C) Both A and B are valid
D) Use single queue with rotation
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B are valid
questions.explanation: Two approaches: costly push (move all) or costly pop (dequeue all but last).

questions.title: Number of Recent Calls (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Count number of pings in last 3000 milliseconds.
options: A) Use queue, push new ping, pop until t - front <= 3000
B) Use list and binary search for left bound
C) Both A and B, A is simple
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, A is simple
questions.explanation: Maintain queue of timestamps; at each ping, enqueue and remove outdated from front.

questions.title: Moving Average from Data Stream (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Calculate moving average of last size elements.
options: A) Use circular array with sum variable
B) Use queue, maintain running sum
D) Store all values and recalculate
questions.explanation: Queue: enqueue, if size > window, dequeue; update sum; average = sum / current size.

questions.title: Find Largest Value in Each Tree Row (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Return largest value per level in binary tree.
options: A) BFS level order, track max per level
B) DFS with level parameter, update max per level
D) Use priority queue per level
questions.explanation: BFS: for each level compute max; DFS: pass level, maintain array maxAtLevel.

questions.title: Cousins in Binary Tree (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Two nodes are cousins if same depth but different parents.
options: A) BFS storing depth and parent for each node
B) DFS depth and parent search
questions.explanation: Traverse tree, record depth and parent; compare after traversal.

questions.title: Merge Two Binary Trees (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Merge two trees by summing overlapping nodes.
options: A) Recursive: merge(t1.left, t2.left) and merge(t1.right, t2.right)
B) BFS using queue with pairs
questions.explanation: Recursive: if both null return null; new node = (t1?val:0)+(t2?val:0); recurse children.

questions.title: Average of Levels in Binary Tree (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Return average of node values per level.
options: A) BFS level order, compute sum and count
questions.explanation: BFS: process each level separately; sum values, divide by level size.

questions.title: Leaf-Similar Trees (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Check if two trees have same leaf sequence.
options: A) DFS to collect leaf values into arrays, compare
B) DFS with generator and compare lazily
D) BFS level order leaf detection
questions.explanation: Perform DFS on each tree, add leaf values to list; compare lists.

questions.title: Sum of Left Leaves (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find sum of all left leaves in binary tree.
options: A) DFS with flag tracking if node is left child
B) BFS with node and isLeft flag
questions.explanation: Recursive: if left child and leaf, add value, else recurse left+right with isLeft flag.

questions.title: Find Bottom Left Tree Value (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find leftmost value in bottom row of binary tree.
options: A) BFS level order, store first element of each level, return last level's first
B) DFS with depth, track maxDepth and value
questions.explanation: BFS: process right to left, last node processed is bottom-left; or DFS with depth tracking.

questions.title: Minimum Depth of Binary Tree (Tech Mahindra)
questions.problem_statement: Find minimum depth of binary tree (shortest root-to-leaf path).
options: A) BFS: first leaf reached gives min depth
B) DFS recursion: if leaf return 1, else 1+min(left,right) (check null cases)
C) Both A and B, BFS stops early
questions.correct_answer: C) Both A and B, BFS stops early
questions.explanation: BFS returns as soon as leaf found; DFS checks both subtrees.
